item.output:
# Phase 2 — Gameplay Subsystems

The wiring layer between a booted `World` and a playable scene: imports, physics,
audio, assets, input, and the coordinate frame. Every API/code block here is
verified against the live SDK. For the engine surface itself (`World.create`,
ECS systems, `world.*` accessors) see `02-world-and-loop.md`; for panels see
`04-spatial-ui.md`; for API signatures see `api-quick-reference.md`.

## Contents

- [three-imports](#three-imports) — import Three.js from `@iwsdk/core`, ESM-only, don't reconstruct enums
- [physics](#physics) — Havok needs `PhysicsShape` + `PhysicsBody`, not just the flag
- [audio](#audio) — `AudioSource` / `AudioUtils`, not raw Web Audio
- [assets](#assets) — `AssetManager.getGLTF` clone-by-default
- [input](#input) — XR gamepad string ids, RayInteractable, haptics, keyboard, dual-input
- [coordinates](#coordinates) — right-handed, -Z forward, +Y up

---

## three-imports

Standard Three.js classes are re-exported from `@iwsdk/core`. For app code,
import standard Three classes from `@iwsdk/core` so they match IWSDK's
Three/super-three build. Keep the template's `three` dependency/override; import
`three/examples/jsm/...` only for addons that `@iwsdk/core` does not re-export.

```typescript
// ✅ CORRECT
import {
  Mesh,
  Group,
  BoxGeometry,
  SphereGeometry,
  CylinderGeometry,
  PlaneGeometry,
  ConeGeometry,
  TorusGeometry,
  MeshStandardMaterial,
  MeshBasicMaterial,
  LineBasicMaterial,
  Color,
  Vector3,
  Quaternion,
  Euler,
  Matrix4,
  Fog,
  AmbientLight,
  PointLight,
  DirectionalLight,
  BufferGeometry,
  Float32BufferAttribute,
  EdgesGeometry,
  LineSegments,
  AdditiveBlending,
  // ... every standard Three.js export is available
} from '@iwsdk/core';

// Avoid for standard Three classes in app code
// import { Mesh } from "three";
```

Also avoid `import * as THREE from "three"`, and never resolve a class with a
dynamic `await import("@iwsdk/core")` — use a normal static named import.

```typescript
// ❌ Wrong — namespace import; types come from vanilla @types/three, not
//    super-three, causing TS2352 / instanceof drift on augmented APIs.
import * as THREE from 'three';
const fog = new THREE.FogExp2(0x000811, 0.25);

// ❌ Wrong — dynamic import as a workaround to "find" a class
world.scene.fog = new (await import('@iwsdk/core')).Fog(0x000811, 5, 25);

// ✅ Right
import { FogExp2, Fog } from '@iwsdk/core';
```

The scaffold wires Three via a package.json npm-alias dependency
(`"three": "npm:super-three@0.181.0"`) — it is **not** a vite alias and **not** an
`overrides` entry, so don't go looking for one to "fix." Leave it as-is.

### ESM-only — no CommonJS

IWSDK apps are pure ESM (`package.json` `"type": "module"`, Vite, `target: "esnext"`).
Use static `import` only. Never `require()`, `module.exports`, `__dirname`, or a
runtime `require()` for lazy loading — these break the Vite/esbuild build.

```typescript
// ❌ breaks the ESM build
const { InputComponent } = require('@iwsdk/core');
```

**Never hand-reconstruct an `@iwsdk/core` export.** Import the real symbol. Enums
like `InputComponent` are **string** enums (`Trigger = 'xr-standard-trigger'`), and
the gamepad API looks buttons up by that string id. A hand-written numeric
stand-in (`const InputComponent = { Trigger: 0, ... }`) type-checks and builds, but
every `getButtonDown`/`getAxesValues` call silently returns `false`/`undefined` —
all XR input goes dead with no error.

```bash
grep -rn "require(\|module.exports\|__dirname" src/ && echo "CJS found — convert to ESM import"
```

### Duplicate `@types/three` (super-three collision)

The template ships `three: "npm:super-three@..."` plus a floating `@types/three: "*"`.
Those two declare conflicting global `three` types, surfacing as cryptic Three.js
type errors. `@iwsdk/core` already bundles its own Three typings. The canonical fix
is already in the starter `tsconfig.json` — keep it:

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "types": [] // ← do NOT delete; this drops the floating @types/three
  }
}
```

`"types": []` stops TS from auto-including the floating `@types/three`; types then
come from `@iwsdk/core`. Do **not** invent a `paths` remap to fix this (core ships
no nested `@types/three`), and do not reach for `as any`/`as unknown` casts. If
`"types"` got dropped from your tsconfig, re-add `"types": []`.

---

## physics

`features.physics: true` only boots the engine. Nothing simulates until an entity
has BOTH `PhysicsShape` and `PhysicsBody`. The flag alone is dead weight.

```typescript
import {
  PhysicsBody,
  PhysicsState,
  PhysicsShape,
  PhysicsShapeType,
  PhysicsManipulation,
} from '@iwsdk/core';

// 1. Mesh -> entity, then add a shape + a body.
const entity = world.createTransformEntity(ballMesh);
entity.addComponent(PhysicsShape, {
  shape: PhysicsShapeType.Sphere,
  dimensions: [0.2, 0.2, 0.2],
});
//   PhysicsShapeType.Auto derives dimensions from the Three.js geometry
//   (Sphere/Box/Cylinder/ConvexHull).
entity.addComponent(PhysicsBody, { state: PhysicsState.Dynamic });
//   Static = walls/floors/rails (immovable, still collide),
//   Dynamic = gravity+forces, Kinematic = you move it, it pushes others.

// 2. Launch a projectile: one-shot impulse OR exact velocity
//    (PhysicsManipulation auto-removes after 1 frame).
entity.addComponent(PhysicsManipulation, { force: [0, 8, -3] }); // impulse at center of mass
entity.addComponent(PhysicsManipulation, { linearVelocity: [5, 0, 0] }); // overrides velocity

// 3. Read back: the system writes the simulated pose into object3D every frame.
const p = entity.object3D!.position; // updated by PhysicsSystem
const vel = entity.getVectorView(PhysicsBody, '_linearVelocity'); // live velocity (Float32Array xyz)
```

Tuning lives on `PhysicsBody`: `linearDamping` / `angularDamping` (drag),
`gravityFactor` (0 = float, >1 = heavy). Reach for Havok when there are collisions,
restitution, stacking, throwing, or rolling. Hand-roll a kinematic update ONLY for
strictly 1-axis/2D motion with no collision response (a slider, a spinning dial) —
do not write a substep-Euler integrator with manual `vel.y += GRAVITY*dt` and
`vel *= -0.6` bounces; that is exactly what the three components above give you,
deterministically. Canonical example: `examples/physics/src/index.ts`.

> If you genuinely need fully custom motion, scaffold `--no-physics` so you don't
> ship a disabled engine (the Havok WASM still bundles into `dist`). Do not enable
> physics and then hand-roll an integrator, and do not disable physics while
> reimplementing gravity by hand.

---

## audio

Do not new up an `AudioContext` and oscillators by hand — that bypasses spatial
audio and the ECS `AudioSystem` lifecycle. Attach an `AudioSource` component and
drive it with `AudioUtils`:

```typescript
import { AudioSource, AudioUtils } from '@iwsdk/core';

const e = world.createTransformEntity(mesh);
e.addComponent(AudioSource, {
  src: '/audio/hit.mp3',
  volume: 0.8,
  positional: true,
});
AudioUtils.play(e); // also: stop/pause via AudioUtils; loop/autoplay are AudioSource fields
```

`positional: true` gives distance-attenuated 3D audio (refDistance/rolloff/cone
fields on the component); `positional: false` is ambient. Canonical example:
`examples/audio/`.

---

## assets

```typescript
// getGLTF returns a fresh scene graph clone by default
// (geometries, materials, and animations remain shared)
const { scene } = AssetManager.getGLTF('myModel')!;
world.scene.add(scene);

// Opt into shared instance (old behavior)
const { scene } = AssetManager.getGLTF('myModel', { shared: true })!;
```

---

## input

For headset-targeted VR apps, XR controller or hand input should be first-class.
Keyboard/mouse should be a browser fallback, not the only supported input path.

Prefer the stateful XR input API over raw `Gamepad` array indexing:

```typescript
import { InputComponent } from '@iwsdk/core';

const rightGamepad = world.input.xr.gamepads.right;
const triggerDown = rightGamepad?.getButtonDown(InputComponent.Trigger);
const squeezeHeld = rightGamepad?.getButtonPressed(InputComponent.Squeeze);
const thumbstick = rightGamepad?.getAxesValues(InputComponent.Thumbstick);
const aPressed = rightGamepad?.getButtonDown(InputComponent.A_Button);
```

`world.input.xr` is **fully typed** (`XRInputManager`); `.gamepads.left/.right` is
`StatefulGamepad | undefined`. No `as any` is ever needed — if TS complains you are
on a pre-0.4 API; upgrade instead of casting. Two gotchas that silently kill input:

- **All `getButton*`/`getAxesValues` take a string id** (the `InputComponent` enum
  value, e.g. `InputComponent.Thumbstick === 'xr-standard-thumbstick'`). Passing a
  **number** silently returns `undefined` (internal Map miss) — `getAxesValues(2)` is
  a bug, not a stick.
- **`getAxesValues(id)` returns `{ x, y } | undefined`**, never an array. Read
  `.x`/`.y`; `[0]`/`[1]` are always `undefined`.

```typescript
const right = world.input.xr.gamepads.right;
if (right) {
  if (right.getButtonDown(InputComponent.Trigger)) fire(); // edge: pressed this frame
  const stick = right.getAxesValues(InputComponent.Thumbstick); // { x, y } | undefined
  if (stick) aim(stick.x, stick.y); // NOT stick[0]/stick[1]
}
```

Raw WebXR gamepad indices are profile-specific fallback knowledge; prefer
`InputComponent` APIs in app code. Do not transfer raw profile indices directly
to the runtime CLI: `iwsdk xr set-gamepad-state` uses its documented synthetic
schema (`0=trigger`, `1=squeeze`, `2=thumbstick`, `3=A/X`, `4=B/Y`, `5=thumbrest`).

### Anti-pattern: raw WebXR session polling / hand-rolled raycaster

Old example templates poll WebXR directly — delete this when scaffolding from one.
Any `(world as any).renderer?.xr?.getSession()` or `getSession().inputSources` in
input code is a red flag (it bypasses controller-profile remapping and the
interaction system).

```typescript
// WRONG — raw session polling + numeric button indices
const session = (world as any).renderer?.xr?.getSession?.();
for (const src of session.inputSources) {
  if (src.gamepad.buttons[0].pressed) fire();
}
```

For pointing at / clicking objects, do **not** raycast by hand. Tag the target entity
with `RayInteractable` (or `PokeInteractable` for touch); the `InputSystem` registers
it as a target and adds transient `Hovered`/`Pressed` tags you query in a system:

```typescript
import { RayInteractable, Pressed } from '@iwsdk/core';

entity.addComponent(RayInteractable);
// in a system over { required: [RayInteractable] }:
if (e.hasComponent(Pressed)) handleClick(e);
```

### Controller world pose (ray & grip spaces)

To attach to a controller or read its world transform (gesture detection, saber
attach), go through the ECS space entities — not raw gamepad pose arrays.

```typescript
// playerSpaceEntities values are Entities; the Object3D is the `.object3D`
// PROPERTY (there is no getObject3D() method).
const grip = world.playerSpaceEntities.gripSpaces.right.object3D; // Object3D | undefined
const pos = new Vector3();
grip?.getWorldPosition(pos); // world-space controller position
grip?.add(saber.group); // parent content directly to the grip space
```

Available spaces: `head`, `raySpaces`, `gripSpaces`, `indexTipSpaces`, each
`{ left, right }`. Spaces are only valid in an active XR session — gate on
`world.input.xr` / session state. Do not write 0.3/0.4 OR-chains (`world.player` is
_not_ removed; pick one path, prefer `playerSpaceEntities[...].object3D`).

### Haptics (rumble)

There is no IWSDK haptics helper — drive the raw WebXR actuator.
`world.input.xr.gamepads[hand].gamepad` is the typed raw `Gamepad`, so only the
non-standard `pulse` call needs a cast:

```typescript
function rumble(hand: 'left' | 'right', intensity = 1, durationMs = 60) {
  const gp = world.input.xr.gamepads[hand]?.gamepad; // typed Gamepad, no cast
  const actuator = gp?.hapticActuators?.[0] as
    | { pulse?: (i: number, ms: number) => void }
    | undefined;
  actuator?.pulse?.(Math.min(intensity, 1), durationMs);
}
```

Do not cast the whole gamepad to `any` — `.gamepad` is already typed; only the
experimental `pulse` call needs narrowing.

### Mutating components at runtime

`Entity` exposes `setValue(Component, key, value)` and `getValue` — there is **no**
`setComponentValue` method (calling it silently no-ops). Built-in components read
their fields live each frame, so update in place — do **not** remove + re-add a
component to force a refresh (e.g. `Follower.offsetPosition` is re-read every update;
change it with `setValue`). To show/hide an entity toggle `entity.object3D!.visible` —
never move panels off-screen (`y: -100`) or strip/re-add `Follower`.

### Keyboard (browser-first)

`world.input.keyboard` is **already** a typed `StatefulKeyboard` — never write
`(world.input as any).keyboard`. The methods are fully typed; the cast only
discards type safety. (`keyboard` is on `world.input`, NOT `world.input.xr`.)

```typescript
world.input.keyboard.getKeyPressed('KeyW'); // held this frame
world.input.keyboard.getKeyDown('Space'); // pressed this frame
world.input.keyboard.getKeyUp('KeyE'); // released this frame
```

### Dual-input pattern

For games that should work in both browser and VR:

```typescript
// In your update loop (inside a System), direct browser fallback:
if (this.input.keyboard.getKeyDown('Space')) {
  fire();
}

// For shared fire/select intent, register explicit keyboard and XR action
// bindings, or use the built-in locomotion actions where applicable.
```

---

## coordinates

Standard Three.js / WebXR conventions apply:

- **Right-handed** coordinate system.
- **-Z is camera forward.** Cameras look along local -Z.
- **+Y is up.**
- The player origin starts at identity; with an unrotated camera, forward is -Z.
- In browser-first mode, the camera starts at the configured position
  (default: `[0, 1.7, 0]` looking toward -Z unless `render.camera` overrides it).

For games with approach lanes (e.g. rhythm games):

- For a lane aligned with camera forward (-Z), spawn far objects at more
  negative Z values.
- Move those objects toward +Z as they approach the player.
- Put the hit zone near the player at a small negative Z (e.g. `z = -2`).

Now I have all the references I need. Let me plan the game:

**Neon Gems VR** — A match-3 swap puzzle game (Bejeweled/Candy Crush style) in the neon holodeck aesthetic.

Core mechanics:
- 8x8 grid of glowing neon gems in 3D space
- 6 gem types with distinct shapes and colors
- Swap adjacent gems (horizontal or vertical) to make matches of 3+
- Gravity pulls gems down, new gems spawn from top
- Cascading matches for combo points
- Special gems from big matches: 4-match = line clear, 5-match = color bomb, L/T = area blast
- Multiple game modes including Classic with move limits, Timed for 90-second sprints, Zen for relaxed play, Endless until no moves remain, Daily Challenges with seeded puzzles, and Puzzle mode with specific objectives
- VR players use a laser pointer to select and swap gems via trigger confirmation, while browser players click to interact
- The holodeck aesthetic features glowing wireframe gems with unique shapes, neon grid floors and ceilings, and particle effects for visual feedback on cascades, scoring, and combos

summary:
Context checkpoint compaction summary:
# Context Checkpoint: IWSDK Daily Build — Neon Gems VR

## Task
Execute the `iwsdk-daily-build` midnight kick-off job for **2026-06-16** (build #51). Build a fresh IWSDK game from scratch, deploy to GitHub Pages under `ellyz2426`, and maintain status files.

## Current Progress

### Status: Early Round 1 — Source code partially written, not yet compiled or deployed

1. **Status file written** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json` with `"status": "building"`
2. **Project scaffolded** by copying `neon-stack` (build #49) and stripping it clean — `npx @iwsdk/create` failed with `fetch failed` due to network restrictions
3. **Package renamed** to `neon-gems`, `index.html` title updated, old source/UI files removed
4. **Game concept chosen: Neon Gems VR** — a match-3 swap puzzle game (first match-3/swap genre in the portfolio). 8×8 grid of neon gem types, swap adjacent gems to match 3+, cascading, combo scoring.
5. **Main source file partially written** at `~/workspace/neon-gems/src/index.ts` (~65KB total across two writes)

### What's in `src/index.ts` so far:
- Complete type definitions, constants (8×8 grid, 6 gem types with distinct shapes)
- `GameStateManager` class with full state, persistence, difficulty scaling
- 40 achievements defined
- `AudioManager` with procedural SFX (match, cascade, swap, select, invalid, gameStart, gameOver, achievement, countdown, drone music)
- Seeded RNG for daily challenges
- Grid management (init, match finding, gravity, refill, valid move check)
- Particle system (120 pool)
- Environment creation (holodeck: floor/ceiling grids, wall, lights, floating decorations, ambient particles, board frame)
- 4 color themes (Neon/Fire/Ice/Vaporwave), 8 gem skins
- `GameSystem` ECS system with `createSystem` pattern and `qualify` event binding for all 14 PanelUI panels
- Full game flow: countdown → playing → game over, with swap animation, cascade processing, scoring
- Mouse click → raycaster → gem selection input
- XR controller input (B button for pause)
- 6 game modes: Classic (move-limited), Timed, Zen, Endless, Daily (seeded), Puzzle (color target)
- Leaderboard persistence

## What Has NOT Been Done Yet

### Critical remaining work:
1. **14 `.uikitml` template files** — None written yet. Need: `title`, `modeselect`, `difficulty`, `hud`, `countdown`, `pause`, `gameover`, `leaderboard`, `achievements`, `settings`, `help`, `toast`, `stats`, `skins`
2. **Install node_modules** — `node_modules` was deleted; need `cp -R../neon-stack/node_modules.` (npm install fails due to network)
3. **Type-check** — `npx tsc --noEmit`
4. **Build** — `npm run build`
5. **Create GitHub repo** — `gh repo create ellyz2426/neon-gems --public --source=. --push`
6. **Deploy to GitHub Pages** — build + copy dist to gh-pages branch + push
7. **Update status file** — Set round 1 data, change status to `"waiting_continuation"`
8. **Update BUILD_LOG.md** and `latest-build-summary.md`
9. **Write README.md** for the game repo
10. **Capture build journal** — copy session JSONL to `.agent/journal/`

## Key Constraints (from task description and SKILL.md)

- **ALL UI must be PanelUI** (`.uikitml` → compiled JSON → `PanelUI` component). Zero HTML DOM for game UI. Only `document.getElementById('app')` for IWSDK container is allowed.
- **ECS System pattern required** — `createSystem` + `world.registerSystem`, NOT `world.onUpdate` or raw rAF
- **Import Three.js from `@iwsdk/core`**, never from `three`
- **PanelUI loads async** — wire handlers via `qualify` event, use `setProperties({ text})` not DOM methods
- **Config paths must be relative** — `'./ui/x.json'` not `'/ui/x.json'`
- **ASCII-only in panel text** — no emoji, no em-dash, no special chars
- **No multi-value padding/margin shorthand** in uikitml
- **XR controller support mandatory** — trigger to interact, B for pause, pointer for menus
- **Dual runtime** — `xr: { offer: 'once'}` + `canvasPointerEvents: true`
- **Node 20.19.0** is available and working
- **GitHub token lacks `workflow` scope** — use manual gh-pages deployment method
- Network-restricted environment — `npx @iwsdk/create` fails, copy node_modules from sibling project

## Previously Built Games (DO NOT repeat concepts)
From BUILD_LOG.md and registry: 50 prior builds covering genres including: Pong, space shooter, rhythm, tower defense, golf, bowling, archery, pool, pinball, darts, hockey, basketball, boxing, sword dueling, racing, target smash, marble maze, baseball, curling, volleyball, table tennis, brick breaker, skee-ball, crane claw, bubble shooter, fishing, artillery, whack-a-mole, memory/Simon, slicing, drop puzzle, Tetris, ring toss, launch, orbit, endless runner, stacking, bowling (lanes). **Match-3 swap puzzle is a NEW genre** — confirmed not in the list.

## Project Structure
```
~/workspace/neon-gems/
├── package.json (name: neon-gems, IWSDK deps via file: links)
├── vite.config.ts (compileUIKit + iwsdkDev plugins, base: './')
├── tsconfig.json (strict, types:[], skipLibCheck)
├── index.html (title: Neon Gems VR)
├── src/index.ts (main game code, ~65KB)
├── ui/ (EMPTY — needs 14.uikitml files)
├── public/ (empty, ui/*.json will be generated by vite plugin)
└──.iwsdk/ (SDK config)
```

## Follower Panel Configuration
From the code written: HUD, countdown, and toast panels use `Follower` targeting `world.player`. World-space panels positioned at `(0, 1.4, -2.2)`.

      <text fontSize="16" fontWeight="bold" color="#ff44ff">MATCH 3 OR MORE</text>

      <text fontSize="16" fontWeight="bold" color="#ffdd00">COMBOS</text>

      <text fontSize="13" color="#ccccee">Point controller at gems and pull trigger to select. Press B to pause.</text>
      <text fontSize="16" fontWeight="bold" color="#ff8844">TIPS</text>

item.name: write
item.arguments:
{"content":"# Neon Gems VR

A match-3 swap puzzle game built with [IWSDK](https://iwsdk.dev) (Immersive Web SDK). Swap adjacent gems on an 8x8 neon grid to match 3 or more, trigger cascading combos, and chase high scores across 6 game modes.

**[Play Now](https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-gems/)**

## Features

- **8x8 neon gem grid** with 6 distinct gem types (Ruby, Sapphire, Emerald, Topaz, Amethyst, Citrine), each with unique 3D shapes
- **6 game modes**: Classic (move-limited), Timed, Zen, Endless, Daily Challenge (seeded), Puzzle (color targets)
- **3 difficulty levels** with scaling move/time limits
- **Cascading match system** with combo multipliers
- **40 achievements** to unlock
- **8 gem skins** (Classic Neon, Bright Pulse, Soft Glow, Prismatic, Deep Core, Chrome, Void, Solar)
- **4 color themes** (Neon, Fire, Ice, Vaporwave)
- **Procedural audio** — synthesized SFX and ambient drone music
- **Persistent leaderboard and statistics** via localStorage
- **Full VR support** — controller pointing + trigger to select, B to pause
- **Browser-first** — mouse click to select/swap gems on desktop
- **14 PanelUI spatial panels** — title, mode select, difficulty, HUD, countdown, pause, game over, leaderboard, achievements, settings, help, toast, stats, skins

## Controls

### Desktop
- Click a gem to select it, click an adjacent gem to swap

### VR
- Point controller at gem, pull trigger to select
- Press B to pause

## Tech Stack

- [IWSDK](https://iwsdk.dev) v0.4.1 — Immersive Web SDK
- Three.js (via super-three)
- PanelUI + uikitml for spatial UI
- ECS architecture with `createSystem`
- Vite build pipeline

## Development

```bash
npm run dev      # Start dev server
npm run build    # Production build
```

## License

MIT
","path":"workspace/neon-gems/README.md"}

A precision block-stacking game built with [IWSDK](https://iwsdk.dev) — playable in the browser and in VR.

## Play

[**Play Now →**](https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-stack/)

## Gameplay

Blocks slide back and forth. Time your placement to stack them as high as possible. Any overhang gets cut off — but land a perfect placement and the block grows back!

### Game Modes

| Mode | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Classic** | Speed increases as you climb |
| **Zen** | No game over — stack forever |
| **Speed** | 60 seconds on the clock |
| **Precision** | No block growth — every cut is permanent |
| **Challenge** | Reach a random target height |
| **Endless** | Constant speed — pure focus |

### Controls

- **VR**: Trigger or A button to place, B button to pause
- **Browser**: Click/tap to place, Space bar to place, Escape to pause

## Features

- 🎮 6 game modes with distinct mechanics
- 🏆 44 achievements to unlock
- 📊 Persistent statistics tracking
- 🎵 Procedural audio engine
- ✨ Dynamic particle effects and tower lighting
- 🌌 Environmental progression — sky shifts as you climb
- 🥽 Full VR support with XR controller input
- 🖱️ Browser click/tap support

## Tech

Built with [IWSDK](https://github.com/nicholasio/immersive-web-sdk) using:
- PanelUI spatial interface system (11 `.uikitml` templates)
- ECS architecture with `createSystem`
- Three.js rendering with neon aesthetics
- Web Audio API for procedural sound

## Development

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

## License

MIT

# Neon Stack VR

A precision block-stacking game built with [IWSDK](https://iwsdk.dev) — playable in the browser and in VR.

## Play

[**Play Now →**](https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-stack/)

## Gameplay

Blocks slide back and forth. Time your placement to stack them as high as possible. Any overhang gets cut off — but land a perfect placement and the block grows back!

### Game Modes

| Mode | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Classic** | Speed increases as you climb |
| **Zen** | No game over — stack forever |
| **Speed** | 60 seconds on the clock |
| **Precision** | No block growth — every cut is permanent |
| **Challenge** | Reach a random target height |
| **Endless** | Constant speed — pure focus |

### Controls

- **VR**: Trigger or A button to place, B button to pause
- **Browser**: Click/tap to place, Space bar to place, Escape to pause

## Features

- 🎮 6 game modes with distinct mechanics
- 🏆 44 achievements to unlock
- 📊 Persistent statistics tracking
- 🎵 Procedural audio engine
- ✨ Dynamic particle effects and tower lighting
- 🌌 Environmental progression — sky shifts as you climb
- 🥽 Full VR support with XR controller input
- 🖱️ Browser click/tap support

## Tech

Built with [IWSDK](https://github.com/nicholasio/immersive-web-sdk) using:
- PanelUI spatial interface system (11 `.uikitml` templates)
- ECS architecture with `createSystem`
- Three.js rendering with neon aesthetics
- Web Audio API for procedural sound

## Development

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

## License

MIT

item.arguments:
{"content":"
## Build #51: Neon Gems VR (AM 2026-06-16)

- **Genre:** Match-3 swap puzzle (NEW genre — first match-3 in portfolio)
- **Concept:** 8x8 grid of neon gems with 6 distinct types (Ruby/Sapphire/Emerald/Topaz/Amethyst/Citrine), each with unique 3D shapes. Swap adjacent gems to match 3+, cascading combos, 6 game modes.
- **Repo:** https://github.com/ellyz2426/neon-gems
- **Live:** https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-gems/
- **LOC:** 1,819 (TypeScript)
- **Panels:** 14 PanelUI templates (title, modeselect, difficulty, hud, countdown, pause, gameover, leaderboard, achievements, settings, help, toast, stats, skins)
- **Achievements:** 40
- **Modes:** 6 (Classic, Timed, Zen, Endless, Daily Challenge, Puzzle)
- **Skins:** 8 gem skins + 4 color themes
- **Build time:** ~30 minutes, 1 round
- **Type errors fixed:** 6 (keyboard/xr input types, Group.material, camera in render config, Follower offsetPosition tuple)
- **Status:** COMPLETE
","mode":"append","path":"workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md"}

{"command":"cat > ~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md << 'EOF'
# Build #51: Neon Gems VR

**Date:** 2026-06-16 (AM cycle)
**Genre:** Match-3 swap puzzle (NEW — first match-3 in portfolio)
**Live:** https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-gems/
**Repo:** https://github.com/ellyz2426/neon-gems

## Stats
- **LOC:** 1,819 TypeScript
- **Panels:** 14 PanelUI (uikitml → compiled JSON)
- **Achievements:** 40
- **Game modes:** 6 (Classic, Timed, Zen, Endless, Daily Challenge, Puzzle)
- **Gem skins:** 8 | **Themes:** 4
- **Build time:** ~30 min, 1 round
- **Type errors fixed:** 6

## Key Features
- 8×8 grid with 6 gem types (Ruby, Sapphire, Emerald, Topaz, Amethyst, Citrine) — each a distinct 3D shape
- Swap adjacent gems to match 3+, cascading gravity refill, combo multipliers
- 3 difficulty levels with scaling parameters
- Seeded RNG for daily challenge mode (same board for everyone)
- Puzzle mode with color-specific gem targets
- Procedural audio engine: 10 SFX types + ambient drone music
- Particle system (120 pool) for match/cascade/combo/achievement effects
- Holodeck environment: floor/ceiling grids, walls, floating decorations, ambient particles
- Persistent leaderboard (top 20) and lifetime stats via localStorage
- VR: controller trigger to select, B to pause
- Browser: mouse click gem selection via raycaster

## Portfolio
- **Total builds attempted:** 51
- **Total completed:** 41
- **Genres covered:** 39+ (match-3/swap is new)
EOF
echo "SUMMARY_OK""}


**When your build round ends**, update the status file:
- Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` (if under 6h) or `"complete"` (if ≥6h)
- Append to `rounds` array: `{"round": N, "start_utc": "...", "end_utc": "...", "minutes": N, "files": N, "lines": N, "features_added": [...]}`
- Update `total_build_minutes` (sum of all rounds)
- Update `features_completed` with everything built so far
- Set `last_updated_utc`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**ALL game UI MUST use IWSDK's built-in PanelUI system. Do NOT use HTML DOM elements for any game UI.**

This is a hard gate. HTML `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, or any DOM overlay for menus, HUDs, scorecards, settings, notifications, or any other game UI is **forbidden**. HTML overlays are invisible in XR and are an anti-pattern.

Instead, use:
- **`.uikitml` templates** in a `ui/` directory, compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- **`PanelUI` component** to attach compiled UI panels to entities
- **`Follower` component** with `world.player.head` target for head-locked HUDs in XR
- **`ScreenSpace` component** for browser-mode overlays that auto-return to world-space in XR
- **World-space panels** for in-scene menus, leaderboards, settings boards

Read the IWSDK dev skill section **4b** for full API details, examples, and patterns.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features or adding content.**

Build order for every game:
1. Core gameplay loop + basic XR controller input
2. All UI as PanelUI spatial panels (menus, HUD, scorecards, settings)
3. **VERIFY in XR:** Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
   - All panels render and are readable in XR
   - XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
   - HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
   - No HTML overlays remain
4. ONLY THEN expand with additional features, content, modes, polish

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Build instructions:

1. **Research and brainstorm** a new game concept. Be creative.

2. **Build it** in `~/workspace/<game-name>/`:
   - Use the latest IWSDK (currently 0.4.1+)
   - Holodeck aesthetic (clean, futuristic, neon wireframe, luminous)
   - **ALL UI via PanelUI** — `.uikitml` templates, `Follower` for HUDs, `ScreenSpace` for overlays. Zero HTML DOM UI.
   - **XR controller support is mandatory** — trigger to fire/interact, grip for grab, pointer for aiming/menus. Must work end-to-end with VR controllers only. Use IWSDK's InputComponent API and action system.
   - Support both VR AND browser-first mode with dual runtime (`xr: { offer: 'once' }` + `browserControls: true`)
   - Include audio (procedural sound effects, music)
   - Full gameplay loop with scoring, progression, polish

3. **Create GitHub repo** under `ellyz2426`:
   ```
   gh repo create ellyz2426/<game-name> --public --source=. --push --description "<description>"
   ```

4. **Deploy to GitHub Pages**:
   ```
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   Then configure Pages:
   ```
   gh api repos/ellyz2426/<game-name>/pages -X PUT -f "build_type=legacy" -F "source[branch]=gh-pages" -F "source[path]=/"
   ```
   Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope.

5. **Push intermediate commits** to main throughout the build, and redeploy to gh-pages at major milestones.

6. **When your round ends**, update the status file, update the build log at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md`, and write to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md`.

7. **Capture build journal** — Copy your raw session JSONL from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo (NOT `.hatch`). Commit and push.

Hourly check-in jobs will monitor your status file. If you stop early, they will continue the build.
